answers.value:
Grace Lutheran Church, Livonia, MI:
- Elder (three 3-year terms: 2008-2010, 2012-2014, 2016-2018). Responsibilities included regular home and hospital visits to shut-ins, assisting pastor with communion distribution, following up with inactive members, and participating in monthly board of elders meetings.
- Adult Bible Study Leader (2010-present). Have led multi-week studies on Romans, Ephesians, the Small Catechism, and topical series on Christian parenting, financial stewardship, and vocation. Typically prepare detailed handouts and encourage open discussion, often drawing on my own experiences as a husband, father, and engineer.
- Chair, Board of Education (2013-2016). Oversaw Sunday School curriculum selection, coordinated teacher training, and helped launch a midweek confirmation support group for parents.
- Men’s Ministry Coordinator (2015-2020). Organized monthly men’s breakfasts, annual retreat at a Christian camp in northern Michigan, and service projects such as fixing widows’ homes, church work days, and partnering with a local food pantry.
- Worship Assistant / Liturgist (2017-present). Assist with readings, prayers, and occasionally leading services of prayer when pastor is away (under his direction).

St. Paul Lutheran Church, Grand Rapids, MI:
- Youth Ministry Volunteer (1994-2002). Helped with high school youth group, chaperoned retreats and National Youth Gatherings, and led small group Bible discussions.
- Adult Bible Class Teacher (1998-2002). Co-taught a Sunday morning class on basic Christian doctrine for new members and those returning to the church after many years away.

Redeemer Lutheran Church, East Lansing, MI:
- Usher (1985-1989). Regularly served on a college student usher team for Sunday services.
- Campus Bible Study Helper (1987-1989). Helped set up for midweek campus Bible study, occasionally led opening prayers and small group discussions.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value:
Additional relevant responsibilities and experiences:

Grace Lutheran Church, Livonia, MI:
- Stephen Ministry Trainee (completed training in 2018). Although our congregation did not formally launch a full Stephen Ministry program, the training significantly shaped how I approach one-on-one care, listening, and confidentiality. I have been informally asked by our pastor to walk alongside a few men going through job loss, marital strain, and health challenges.
- Evangelism / Outreach Committee Member (2011-2015). Participated in neighborhood canvassing, planning of seasonal outreach events (Trunk-or-Treat, Christmas concerts, VBS), and follow-up visits with visitors and families who attended our preschool open houses. I often paired with another member to make in-home or coffee-shop visits.
- Vacation Bible School (VBS) Bible Story Leader (2012-2019). Wrote skits, recruited volunteers, and coordinated daily Bible story stations for groups of 20–30 children at a time. I made a point to learn children’s names, follow up with parents, and invite unchurched families to worship.

Circuit and District Involvement:
- Circuit Lay Delegate to Michigan District Convention (2015, 2018, 2022). Represented Grace Lutheran, participated in floor committees, and reported back to the congregation and council about resolutions and district priorities.
- Participant in Michigan District Deacon Workshops (2017-2019). Attended several Saturday workshops on pastoral care, Lutheran doctrine, and congregational leadership as part of my deacon formation.

Personal Ministries Informally Connected to the Congregation:
- Marriage Mentoring with My Wife, Laura (2016-present). Together we have met with several engaged or newly married couples at the pastor’s request. We share openly about our own struggles (e.g., balancing work, parenting, and church responsibilities; communication differences; finances) and how the Gospel has sustained our marriage.
- Hospital and Shut-In Visitation (2008-present). Even before being officially installed as a deacon, I accompanied our pastor on visits and then began making some visits on my own with his guidance, especially to older men who appreciate having another man to talk with about faith, fears, and end-of-life questions.
